name: Hansard (APH)
description: The official record of everything said in the Australian Parliament — debates, question time, speeches, and votes. Available free online through the Parliament of Australia website.
whyUseIt: Hansard is the primary source for political research. It records exactly what politicians say in parliament, making it invaluable for opposition research, policy analysis, speech preparation, and accountability. If a politician said it in parliament, it is in Hansard.
marketerWorkflow: Search for debates on your target issue, review what each party said, identify useful quotes for media releases, track how positions have evolved over time
managerExpects: Staff who can quickly find what any MP said about any issue, when they said it, and how it compares to their current position

vocabulary.definition: The near-verbatim official transcript of parliamentary proceedings, named after its original British printer
The daily session where MPs question ministers about government policy and administration

beginnerMistakes: Quoting a politician out of context — always read the full exchange
Not using ParlInfo's advanced search features — simple keyword searches miss a lot
